name: Eggs Neptune with Orange Hollandaise
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Cooked crab meat, fresh or canned
4 Thin slices Orange, cut in half (optional)
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the Hollandaise sauce and keep it warm. Mix water with vinegar in a wide, shallow saucepan. Add salt to the mixture. Bring water to boil, then reduce heat to gentle simmer. One at a time, break each egg into a separate small dish or bowl; gently slip them into the pan without overcrowding. Continue simmering until whites are firmly set and yolks are slightly opaque, about 3 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to carefully remove each egg to a folded kitchen towel to drain. Trim any ragged edges from whites with a small kitchen knife.
While poaching eggs, melt half the butter in a large skillet over moderate heat and sauté crab meat until heated through. Split and toast English muffins. Spread remaining butter on cut sides of muffins. Place two halves on each serving plate. Top each half with crab meat and a poached egg. Spoon or pour Hollandaise sauce over eggs to cover, spreading down the side of the muffin onto the plate. Garnish with paprika and an orange slice if desired. Makes 4 servings.
Orange Hollandaise Sauce:
In a small saucepan, melt butter over low heat until liquid. Place remaining ingredients in a food processor or blender and process until smooth. With machine running, pour melted butter slowly into mixture until fully incorporated. Transfer sauce to a metal or glass bowl, keeping it warm by placing the bowl in hot water. Makes about 1 1/4 cups.
